instruction: Add concise docstrings to every public function in src/main.py describing what it does, its parameters, and its return value.
Add type hints to all method signatures in src/main.py, including the Stack class and any internal helpers. Do not change behavior.
Add input validation that raises TypeError if either argument is not a list, and add a test for it.
Log each evaluated expression and its result to stdout using Python's logging module at INFO level, and add a test that checks the logged output.
Refactor src/main.py to use str.casefold() instead of lower() for case normalization, and add a test verifying it handles the Turkish dotless i correctly.
Refactor the CSV parser in src/main.py to use a single pass over the input string without calling splitlines(), and ensure all existing tests still pass.
Rename the method add_edge(u, v) to connect(u, v) throughout src/main.py and tests/test_main.py, preserving all behavior.
Replace the '**' operator support with a '^' operator that has the same precedence and right-associativity, and update the tests accordingly.
Add a __repr__ method to TokenBucket that shows capacity, refill_rate, and current tokens, and add a test for it.
Add an __iter__ method to MinHeap that yields items in non-decreasing order without modifying the heap, and add tests.
Add optional timeout parameters to put(item, timeout=None) and get(timeout=None) that raise queue.Full or queue.Empty on timeout, and add tests.
Extend rle_encode and rle_decode to also accept and return bytes when the input is bytes, while keeping str support unchanged. Add tests for bytes round-tripping.
Rewrite the make_palindrome implementation to use a clean recursive helper instead of the current algorithm, and ensure all tests still pass.
Generalize encode_cyclic and decode_cyclic to accept a group_size parameter (default 3) and correctly cycle/decode groups of any positive size. Update and add tests.
Change triples_sum_to_zero to return the actual sorted tuple of three indices whose values sum to zero, or None if no such triple exists. Update tests.
Rewrite smallest_change using dynamic programming with memoization instead of the current approach, keeping the same public signature and test results.
Refactor closest_integer to use the decimal module for parsing and rounding instead of float(), ensuring the tie-breaking rule still works. Add tests with very long decimal strings.
Add a new function get_spiral_order(grid: list[list[int]]) -> list[int] that returns elements in clockwise spiral order, plus tests. Keep get_row unchanged.
Change sort_array to sort the input list in place and return None, and update the tests to call it with a copy and assert the original is mutated.
Extend the module with a new function top_n_prime_digit_sums(numbers: list[int], n: int) -> list[int] that returns the digit sums of the top n largest primes in descending order. Add tests.
